intro: The pivotal question between OpenX and Dable is how much of your setup stays portable: OpenX carries low lock-in and Dable carries high lock-in. OpenX is programmatic ad exchange and supply-side platform for publishers. Dable is native content-recommendation ad network, strong in Asia. Pick OpenX when the job is large publishers wanting another demand source in their header bidding wrapper and you accept low lock-in; pick Dable when it is content publishers with significant kr, jp, or southeast asian audience and you accept high lock-in. The data we have shows OpenX at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience, and Dable at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — OpenX is a poor fit for mobile-only app publishers needing sdk-first tooling and app-specific demand, and Dable is a poor fit for western publishers or apps needing video, rewarded, or banner formats. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: OpenX is for programmatic ad exchange and supply-side platform; Dable is for native content-recommendation ad network; decide based on whether you can accept sales-call pricing.
verdict_a: Choose OpenX if keeping the configuration portable matters and the work maps to programmatic ad exchange and supply-side platform for publishers.
verdict_b: Choose Dable if you can tolerate some setup friction and the work lines up with native content-recommendation ad network, strong in Asia.

faq.a: Neither OpenX nor Dable publishes entry pricing that lets us compare directly — OpenX uses a custom model and Dable uses a custom model. Get quotes from both before assuming one is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from OpenX to Dable?
faq.a: Moving from OpenX to Dable is feasible, but Dable is rated high lock-in — expect your integrations and workflows to need rework to match its model. Budget for a parallel-run period rather than a hard cutover.

Both OpenX and Dable rate the same on developer experience (3/5). The decision on DX then comes down to taste — which CLI, UI, or workflow matches your team's habits. A short side-by-side trial is the quickest way to tell.

faq.q: Is Dable a good alternative to OpenX?
faq.a: Yes — Dable is a reasonable alternative to OpenX for large publishers wanting another demand source in their header bidding wrapper.. The practical differences are custom-vs-custom billing and low-vs-high lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Dable as a credible swap.
